message:
fix(channel): prevent double-panic abort in mpsc channel Drop impls

Apply the same defensive locking pattern from the watch channel fix to
the mpsc channel. All `.expect("channel lock poisoned")` calls inside
Drop implementations are replaced with `if let Ok(...)` / `let Ok(...)
else { return }` to prevent a second panic during stack unwinding when
a mutex is already poisoned.

Affected Drop impls:
- Reserve::drop: bail early on poisoned inner lock; use defensive
  locking on waiter mutex when propagating capacity wakes (replaces
  `take_next_sender_waker()` with direct `.lock().ok()` on the waiter)
- Sender::drop: bail early on poisoned inner lock instead of panicking
- SendPermit::drop: bail early on poisoned inner lock; use defensive
  locking for capacity wake propagation
- Receiver::drop: bail early on poisoned inner lock; use `filter_map`
  with `.lock().ok()` when draining send_wakers to skip poisoned waiter
  mutexes instead of panicking

Add 4 regression tests:
- sender_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic
- receiver_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic
- reserve_future_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic
- send_permit_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic

Each test intentionally poisons the channel's inner mutex via
catch_unwind, then verifies that dropping the corresponding channel
handle completes without triggering a double-panic abort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
